serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 g, equivalent to one restaurant plate of green mango prawn salad.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Shrimp, mixed species, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID 175180) and Raw mango (FDC ID 169910), combined component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for green mango prawn salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: cooked prawns (~50% by weight), raw green mango (~30%), dressing ingredients including fish sauce, lime juice, chilli, shallots, peanuts (~20%). Energy, macros, sodium, and fibre are weighted composites. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate for a typical 100 g portion of this salad.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for green mango prawn salad. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous mixed salad with low-GI fruit (green/unripe mango GI ~41–51 per University of Sydney GI Database for unripe mango) and protein-dominant prawn component which suppresses glycemic response. Composite dish GI estimated conservatively at 45 (low). GL per 250 g serving = (45 × 9.8 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from fish sauce and dressing
hypertension.guidance: A 250 g serving provides approximately 1050 mg sodium, which represents a substantial portion of the 1500–2000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced fish sauce or consume smaller portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 45 and approximately 24.5 g carbohydrate per 250 g serving, this salad is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as part of a balanced meal, though the natural fruit sugars from green mango should be accounted for in carbohydrate counting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean protein from prawns, low saturated fat, and antioxidant-rich ingredients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Green mango prawn salad provides lean protein with low saturated fat (approximately 1.5 g per serving) and antioxidants from mango and herbs, making it a heart-friendly option; however, the sodium content from fish sauce warrants attention for patients with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green Mango Prawn Salad is a vibrant Southeast Asian-inspired salad dish popular in Singapore, featuring julienned or shredded unripe green mango as the primary fruit base combined with cooked prawns. The salad is dressed with a tangy, spicy, and savoury combination of lime juice, fish sauce, chilli, and shallots, and is typically garnished with crushed peanuts and fresh herbs such as coriander or mint. The unripe mango provides a firm, crunchy texture and a sour-tart flavour profile that contrasts with the sweet, succulent prawns. The dish is served chilled or at room temperature and is commonly consumed as a starter or light main course. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-calorie, high-protein, low-fat dish with a low glycemic index, making it suitable for weight-conscious individuals and those managing blood glucose. The sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. The dish is rich in vitamin C from the green mango and provides vitamin B12 and selenium from the prawns. It is classified as minimally processed and is a good source of lean protein for individuals following a balanced diet.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for green mango prawn salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked prawns and raw green mango with dressing ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate based on unripe mango GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (cooked prawns FDC 175180, raw mango FDC 169910, fish sauce FDC 173610, peanuts FDC 172430). No direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry was found for this dish. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources for this dish type and is not expected to be a significant contributor. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero) as prawns and plant-based ingredients do not contain industrially produced trans fats. GI is an analogous estimate at 45 (low) based on unripe mango GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and the protein-suppression effect of prawns on glycemic response. Sodium is elevated due to fish sauce in the dressing; the 420 mg per 100 g estimate reflects a typical restaurant-style dressing application. The dish is served at Viet'Spice restaurant on Circular Road, Singapore, suggesting Vietnamese-influenced preparation style which may include additional herbs. Allergens include crustaceans (prawns), fish (fish sauce), and peanuts. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients but cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification. Gluten-free status assumes no soy sauce is used in the dressing.
